experiments.title: Chemistry Equipment – Full Set
experiments.aim: To study common laboratory equipment used in chemistry.
experiments.theory: Chemistry experiments require various apparatus such as beakers, flasks, test tubes, and burners for safe and accurate experimentation.
procedure: Observe the 3D model of laboratory equipment.
Understand the use of each equipment.
experiments.observation: Different laboratory apparatus are clearly visible.
experiments.result: Students can identify and understand the use of chemistry lab equipment.

experiments.title: Volcano Chemical Reaction
experiments.aim: To demonstrate a chemical reaction using a volcano model.
experiments.theory: Some chemical reactions release energy in the form of heat and gas, known as exothermic reactions.
procedure: Understand how gas formation causes eruption.
Relate it to baking soda and vinegar reaction.
experiments.result: Exothermic reactions are understood through visualization.

experiments.title: Condenser
experiments.aim: To study the working of a condenser.
experiments.theory: A condenser cools vapour and converts it into liquid during distillation.
experiments.observation: Cooling tubes and flow path are visible.
experiments.result: The working of a condenser is understood.

experiments.title: Acid-Base Titration
experiments.description: Simulation of an acid-base titration process
experiments.aim: To study the setup and process of acid-base titration.
experiments.theory: Titration involves adding a known concentration of titrant to a solution of unknown concentration (analyte) until the equivalence point is reached, often indicated by a color change.
procedure: Observe the titration apparatus setup (burette, stand, conical flask).
Understand the role of the titrant and analyte.
Note the color change at the equivalence point.
experiments.observation: The apparatus setup is visible, and the process of drop-wise addition is understood.
experiments.result: The concept of determining unknown concentration through titration is understood.
precautions: Handle virtual acids and bases with care.
Ensure accurate reading of the burette meniscus.
